q: Incrementally replacing a legacy monolith by routing selected paths to new microservices through a gateway is which pattern?
e: Strangler Fig migrates functionality slice by slice behind a routing facade until the monolith is retired.

q: Mobile and web clients need differently shaped APIs. Which pattern avoids one bloated general-purpose API?
e: BFF gives each client type its own tailored backend, often implemented at/behind the gateway.

q: A downstream dependency is failing; you want to stop hammering it and fail fast until it recovers. Pattern?
e: Circuit breakers trip after failures, short-circuit calls, and probe for recovery — preventing cascade.

q: Which pattern pairs naturally WITH circuit breaker for transient faults?
o: Retry with exponential backoff and jitter
e: Retry handles transient blips; the breaker stops retries when failure is persistent.

q: Isolating resource pools so one tenant's overload cannot exhaust capacity for others is which pattern?
e: Bulkheads partition connections/compute per consumer/workload, containing failures.

q: Separating the read model from the write model, often with different stores, is…
e: CQRS splits command and query paths; frequently combined with event sourcing.

q: Storing state changes as an append-only sequence of events replayed to build state is…
e: Event sourcing keeps the event log as the source of truth; projections build read models.

q: An app loads data into Redis on cache miss and invalidates on update. Which pattern?
e: Cache-aside (lazy loading): check cache → miss → load from store → populate cache.

q: Combining multiple backend calls into one client round trip at the gateway is…
e: Aggregation reduces chattiness for clients; routing directs requests; offloading moves cross-cutting concerns.

q: Terminating TLS and handling auth at APIM instead of in each microservice is which gateway pattern?
e: Offloading centralizes cross-cutting functionality (TLS, auth, throttling) at the gateway.

q: Deploying identical isolated stamps of the full stack per tenant group or region is…
e: Stamps are repeatable scale/isolation units; geodes are active-active regional nodes behind global routing.

q: A translation layer preventing a new system from being polluted by legacy models/protocols is…
e: ACL isolates bounded contexts, translating between new and legacy semantics.

q: Dapr running alongside each container app providing mTLS, retries, and pub/sub is an example of which pattern?
e: Dapr is a sidecar: co-located helper extending the app without code coupling.

q: Health probes exposed by services so load balancers and orchestrators can gate traffic is which pattern?
e: Health endpoints let infrastructure verify functional status, not just process liveness.

q: Two services in series have 99.95% and 99.9% SLAs. Approximate composite availability?
e: Serial dependencies multiply: 0.9995 × 0.999 ≈ 0.99850 → 99.85%.

q: Deploying an instance in two independent regions each with 99.9% availability behind global failover yields roughly…
e: Parallel redundancy: 1−(1−0.999)² = 99.9999% theoretical ceiling (real-world lower due to failover mechanics).

q: RPO of 15 minutes means…
o: Up to 15 minutes of data loss is acceptable
e: RPO = max tolerable data loss window; RTO = max tolerable downtime.

q: Which Well-Architected pillar does 'use IaC, safe deployment practices, and observability' map to?
e: Operational Excellence covers DevOps practices, automation, and monitoring of operations.

q: A solution must survive a datacenter failure within a region at minimal redesign. Recommend:
e: Zone redundancy protects against datacenter-level failure within the region.

q: Which AZ-305 domain carries the highest weight (April 2026 blueprint)?
e: Design infrastructure solutions is 30–35%; identity/governance and data storage are 25–30% each; BC is 10–15%.

q: What is the prerequisite certification for the Azure Solutions Architect Expert credential?
e: Passing AZ-305 plus holding AZ-104 earns the Expert certification.

q: Company policy: minimize cost; workload is a dev/test API with sporadic traffic tolerating cold starts. Best APIM tier?
e: Consumption is serverless pay-per-call with scale-to-zero — cheapest for sporadic traffic; exam logic = cheapest meeting requirements.

q: 'You need to recommend a messaging solution that guarantees FIFO and supports transactions.' Recommend:
e: Only Service Bus offers sessions (FIFO) plus transactions among these options.

q: A case study requires 'no secrets in code or config anywhere.' Which combination satisfies it end to end?
o: Managed identities + Entra RBAC data roles + Key Vault (MI-accessed) for third-party secrets
e: MI removes Azure-to-Azure secrets; Key Vault (accessed by MI) holds unavoidable external secrets.

q: Requirement: global users, HTTP app, sub-50ms static content latency, WAF, cheapest single service. Recommend:
o: Traffic Manager + App GW per region
e: Front Door combines global anycast entry, caching/CDN, WAF, and origin failover in one service.

q: Monitoring design: you need KQL queries across app traces and platform logs with alerting. Recommend:
o: Azure Monitor with Log Analytics workspace + App Insights, alert rules & action groups
e: Log Analytics is the KQL store; App Insights feeds APM data; alert rules + action groups automate response.

q: Which tool estimates monthly cost of a proposed architecture before deployment?
e: Pricing Calculator models planned architectures; TCO compares on-prem migration economics; Cost analysis tracks actual spend.

q: Steady-state 24/7 production VMs for 3 years, minimizing cost, no flexibility needed. Recommend:
e: Reservations give the deepest discount for predictable long-running compute; Spot is for interruptible work.

q: Which service provides recommendations across cost, security, reliability, performance, and operational excellence?
e: Advisor surfaces WAF-aligned recommendations; Defender focuses on security posture.

q: A regulated workload needs customer-managed keys, double encryption, and immutable audit logs. Which services combine?
o: Key Vault (or Managed HSM) CMK + infrastructure encryption + immutable storage/Log Analytics export
e: CMK via Key Vault/HSM, infrastructure (double) encryption, and immutability policies satisfy common compliance asks.

q: Design for 'API platform must remain available during regional outage; writes must continue.' Data tier suggestion:
o: Cosmos DB multi-region writes (or SQL failover groups for RW failover)
e: Multi-region write (Cosmos) or auto-failover groups keep write availability across regional failure.

q: Hybrid identity: users must sign in with the same password on-prem and cloud, minimal infrastructure, cloud auth. Recommend:
e: PHS is the simplest, most resilient hybrid auth; PTA/ADFS add infrastructure and failure modes.

q: Which assessment should you take (free, official) to gauge AZ-305 readiness?
o: Microsoft Learn Practice Assessment for AZ-305
e: Microsoft's free official practice assessment mirrors exam style; braindumps violate NDA and teach wrong answers.

q: In case-study questions, the most reliable answering strategy is:
o: Map explicit requirements/constraints to the cheapest compliant service; ignore unstated needs
e: AZ-305 rewards requirement-driven minimal designs — never gold-plate beyond stated constraints.

q: 'Recommend a solution to enforce org-wide resource naming and tagging.' Best answer:
o: Azure Policy (deny/append/modify) at management group scope
e: Policy is the governance engine for configuration standards; RBAC cannot express naming rules.

q: Which migration framework phase sequence does Microsoft's Cloud Adoption Framework define?
o: Strategy → Plan → Ready → Adopt (Migrate/Innovate) → Govern + Manage
e: CAF: Strategy, Plan, Ready (landing zones), Adopt, with Govern and Manage as ongoing disciplines.

q: An architecture review finds a single APIM Developer-tier instance serving production traffic. Biggest issue?
o: No SLA on Developer tier — production requires Basic+ (and redundancy for critical apps)
e: Developer tier carries no SLA and is licensed for evaluation/dev/test — a classic exam trap.

q: To protect an APIM-fronted API platform against OWASP Top 10 web attacks, add:
o: WAF (Front Door Premium or App Gateway WAF_v2) in front of APIM
e: APIM is not a WAF; layer a WAF at the edge for OWASP rule-set protection.

q: Requirement: 'audit all privileged role activations and require approval for Owner.' Combine:
o: PIM (eligible assignments, approval, audit) + Entra audit logs
e: PIM handles JIT activation with approvals; audit logs record activations for compliance.

q: Which two artifacts should anchor every AZ-305 design recommendation?
o: Well-Architected Framework pillars + Cloud Adoption Framework guidance
e: Microsoft explicitly frames the architect role around WAF and CAF alignment.
